clinical_history: 62-year-old female. Can you relate the arrhythmia to the other findings? Note: right-sided chest leads!
diagnosis: The rhythm is sinus with long, atypical AV Wenckebach cycles (e.g., probable 6:5 block). The reason for the type 1 second degree AV block is apparent from the rest of the tracing which shows classic findings of an acute inferior/right ventricular myocardial infarction, consistent with proximal to mid-occlusion of the right coronary artery (RCA). The finding of ST elevation in lead III>II, as present here, is also consistent with this finding (see Zimetbaum et al. Am J Cardiol 1998;81:918).
